services.name: Model Explorer UI
services.description: Browse all 27 data model layers, filter and search objects, navigate entity relationships. Hosted in portal-face of 31-eva-faces.

services.name: Model Graph Explorer
services.description: Interactive force-directed rendering of GET /model/graph/ (304 nodes, 533 edges, 20 edge types). Supports BFS traversal, depth filter, edge-type legend. Hosted in portal-face.

services.name: Model Admin Panel
services.description: PUT objects to any of the 27 layers, manage row_version, trigger export/seed/assemble operations, view audit trail by actor + timestamp. Restricted to admin-face. Requires admin Bearer token.

services.name: Model Drift Dashboard
services.description: Displays output of sync-from-source.ps1 and coverage-gaps.ps1: staleness signals, objects missing repo_line, layers lacking source coverage. Surfaced as read-only panel in admin-face.

services.name: Model Impact Analysis View
services.description: Renders GET /model/impact/?container=X as an interactive dependency tree. Shows upstream/downstream blast radius of changing a container, service, or endpoint. Hosted in portal-face.

services.name: Model Sync Agent
services.description: Consumes source diffs (ADO commits, GitHub PRs) and proposes JSON patches to model layers via PUT /model/{layer}/{id}. Routes through 48-eva-orchestrator truth engine. Triggered by CI or scheduled scan.

services.name: Model Drift Detection Agent
services.description: Scheduled agent (hourly/daily). Calls sync-from-source and coverage-gaps scripts, surfaces deltas as ADO work items or webhook alerts. Feeds model-drift-dashboard.

services.name: Model Documentation Generator Agent
services.description: Reads 27 model layers via GET /model/{layer}/ and generates architecture docs, ADO work item descriptions, README stubs, and human-readable reports. Part of 01-documentation-generator pipeline.

services.name: Model Architecture Diagram Agent
services.description: Reads GET /model/graph/ and generates Mermaid diagrams: C4 context, layer dependency, screen flow, agent orchestration. Outputs .md files to docs/ or pushes to ADO wiki.

services.name: PoC Status Agent
services.description: Reads all 48 project STATUS.md files + model layers + ADO sprint data to produce a cross-project health dashboard. Feeds 40-eva-control-plane evidence packs and the ADO Dashboard (39-ado-dashboard).

services.name: Model Trust Linker
services.description: Reads the model's services + endpoints layers to build the trust topology consumed by 47-eva-mti trust computation. Registers trust edges as model relationships. Called during 19-ai-gov Decision Engine Step 5.

services.name: Azure MAF Orchestration Agent
services.description: The orchestration bridge between ADO approval and GitHub Actions execution. Receives dispatch from 38-ado-poc ADO Pipeline. Triggers GitHub workflow_dispatch on project repos. Monitors GH Actions runs. Relays heartbeats to ADO WI comments. Sends completion signal (run_id, pr_url, mti_score, gh_commit_sha) back to ADO Stage 4.
services.notes: Hosted in 29-foundry orchestration plane. Replaces direct ADO->GitHub coupling. Four tasks: trigger-github, monitor-run, relay-heartbeats, completion-signal. Retries: 3x with exponential backoff. Timeout: 6h per run. Registered 2026-02-24.

schemas.notes: Mandatory FinOps attribution. Passed to AuditLogger on every agent invocation.
Lane A-compliant tool invocation record — no raw content/params stored.

schemas.description: Work Breakdown Structure node. Supports dual-mode: Agile Scrum delivery tracked via sprint metrics + classic PM/Gantt tracked via EVM (Earned Value Management). Every WBS node can be reported on independently or rolled up to programme level.

fields.name: owner
fields.description: Person accountable for this WBS node

fields.name: status
fields.description: planned | active | in_progress | done | on_hold | cancelled

fields.name: milestone
fields.description: True if this node is a phase gate or milestone

fields.name: phase_gate
fields.description: WBS-id that must be done first

fields.name: actual_start
fields.description: Gantt: actual start (set when work begins)

fields.name: baseline_start
fields.description: Locked at sprint-0 kickoff, never changes. Variance = planned_start - baseline_start

fields.name: baseline_end
fields.description: Locked at sprint-0 kickoff, never changes

fields.name: sprints_planned
fields.description: Total sprints allocated to this WBS node

fields.name: percent_complete
fields.description: 0-100. Rolled up from stories_done/stories_total for projects. Set manually for streams/program.

fields.name: stories_total
fields.description: Total stories in scope for this WBS node

fields.name: velocity
fields.description: Average story points per sprint (rolling 3-sprint average)

fields.name: cycle_time_days
fields.description: Average calendar days from story In Progress to Done

fields.name: budget_at_completion
fields.description: EVM: BAC -- total authorised budget in story points

fields.name: planned_value
fields.description: EVM: PV -- budgeted cost of work scheduled to date

fields.name: earned_value
fields.description: EVM: EV -- budgeted cost of work actually performed

fields.name: actual_cost
fields.description: EVM: AC -- actual effort expended (story points or person-hours)

fields.name: spi
fields.description: EVM: SPI = EV/PV. <1 = behind schedule. Computed, stored for reporting.

fields.name: cpi
fields.description: EVM: CPI = EV/AC. <1 = over budget. Computed, stored for reporting.

fields.name: estimate_at_completion
fields.description: EVM: EAC = BAC/CPI. Projected total cost.

fields.name: variance_at_completion
fields.description: EVM: VAC = BAC - EAC. Positive = under budget.

fields.name: ado_epic_id
fields.description: ADO Epic work item ID (readback from 38-ado-poc import)

fields.name: ado_feature_id
fields.description: ADO Feature WI if WBS maps to a Feature

fields.name: work_items
fields.description: ADO WI IDs for stories tracked under this node

endpoints.description: Return current authenticated user identity, active persona, assigned roles, and feature flags.
List all available personas the current user may select from at login. Filtered by role membership.
Initiate EVA login. Returns MSAL redirect URL or token if already authenticated via Entra ID.
Terminate the current EVA session and clear persona selection.
Set or update the active persona for the current session. Persists preference. Affects navigation layout, feature flags, and default route returned to the portal.
Main EVA DA chat endpoint. Accepts message + session_id + rag_mode (semantic|keyword|hybrid|reranked|multihop). Streams response via SSE. Replaces /api/conversation in jp-spark.
List available RAG modes for the current user/tenant: semantic, keyword, hybrid, re-ranked, multi-hop. Mode availability gated by feature flags.
Upload one or more files into the EVA DA knowledge base. Accepts multipart/form-data. Triggers async ingestion pipeline. Returns ingestion job_id.
List all knowledge base folders available to the current user (mirrors /api/getfolders).
Create a new knowledge base folder. Folder names are unique per tenant.
List all tags in the knowledge base for the current tenant (mirrors /api/gettags).
Get ingestion status for recent uploads (mirrors /api/uploadstatus as a paginated list with job_id, filename, state, progress %).
Scrape a public URL and ingest into knowledge base. Supports preview mode (returns extracted text + metadata without indexing) and submit mode.
Browse the ingested knowledge base: paginated list of documents with folder, tags, ingestion_date, chunk_count. Filter by folder/tag. Supports keyword-in-metadata search.
Tabular data analysis: upload CSV/Excel + natural-language question, returns streamed narrative + base64 chart images (mirrors /api/tda/analyse).
Retrieve generated chart images for a prior analysis run (mirrors /api/tda/images).
Retrieve the full analysis output (narrative + charts) for a completed run (mirrors /api/tda/output).
Return max allowed CSV file size for analysis upload (mirrors /api/tda/maxcsvfilesize).
Translate a document file into a target language and write result back to designated folder (mirrors /api/translatefile). Async; returns job_id.
Admin endpoint: list all collected chat feedback (rating, comment, session_id, user_hash, timestamp). Paginated. Filter by date/rating. Admin + auditor roles.
Export feedback as CSV for AI governance review cycle (19-ai-gov). Admin + auditor roles.
List all registered accessibility themes (high-contrast, large-text, reduced-motion, etc.).
Create a new accessibility theme with Fluent UI token overrides.
Update an existing accessibility theme. Partial update (merge-patch semantics).
Delete a custom accessibility theme. Blocked if the theme is currently active.
Return the currently active system-wide accessibility theme token set.
Return the full RACI responsibility matrix: all roles x all actions with R/A/C/I designations. Powers RbacResponsibilitiesPage.

Begin an Act-As session. Admin/support role assumes the identity of target_user_id for duration_minutes (default 30). Returns act_as_token. All subsequent API calls made with this token are audit-logged with both real + assumed identity.
End the active Act-As session immediately. Clears the act_as_token and restores the caller's own identity.
Application/structural system logs (INFO/WARN/ERROR/FATAL). Filter by service, log level, time range. Paginated. Second logging lane vs audit logs (user-action / PIPEDA scope).
Export system logs as JSONL or CSV with the same filters as GET /v1/logs/system.
Return all literals belonging to the specified screen ID, in all registered languages. Powers AdminI18nByScreenPage screen-scoped editor.
Bulk import translations from a CSV upload. CSV format: key,language,value. Validates all keys exist in the literals model before applying. Returns validation_errors + applied_count.
List red teaming test results across all runs: per-prompt pass/fail, vulnerability category (OWASP LLM Top 10), severity, timestamp. Used by RedTeamingPage.
List all red-team test run records with summary stats (pass%, fail_count, critical_count).
Trigger a new Promptfoo red-team run against the current EVA DA chat endpoint. Returns run_id. Async; poll GET /v1/redteam/runs/{run_id} for status.
Return current Promptfoo test suite configuration (plugins enabled, target model, threshold settings).
Citizen-facing AssistMe chat: no authentication required for citizen persona. Returns streamed bilingual (EN/FR) response. Limited to curated knowledge base (citizen topics).
List curated topic cards for the AssistMe citizen portal. Returns topic_id, title_en, title_fr, icon, description.
Submit citizen feedback on an AssistMe response. Appends to the same feedback store as EVA DA feedback.

requirements.title: Add 'docs' layer to EVA Data Model for document and deliverable metadata
requirements.notes: Motivation: EVA State Paper (19-ai-gov D-15) had to be registered as a requirements entry (REQ-019-D15) because the model has no docs/deliverables layer. This is a workaround that conflates two distinct concerns. Proposed layer name: 'docs'. Minimum fields: id (e.g. DOC-019-001), title, type (paper|spec|runbook|decision|diagram), project, status (draft|review|published|archived), path (repo-relative file path), format (md|pdf|docx), author, sprint, tags, parent_doc, is_active. This layer would cleanly host: position papers, architecture specs, design decisions, runbooks, and any other project deliverable that today has no dedicated home in the model. Raised: 2026-02-24 after 19-ai-gov Phase 7 (EVA State Paper) work.

prompts.notes: Validates EI eligibility based on insurable hours, job separation reason, and waiting period rules.

security_controls.label: Prompt Injection
security_controls.description: An attacker manipulates a large language model through crafted inputs, causing it to execute unintended actions or disclose unauthorized data.
security_controls.notes: Mitigated via system prompt hardening and input sanitization. Tested in 36-red-teaming via Promptfoo.

security_controls.label: Insecure Output Handling
security_controls.description: Insufficient validation of LLM outputs before passing downstream, enabling XSS, CSRF, SSRF, or remote code execution.
security_controls.notes: Output sanitization layer planned for Sprint 7.

security_controls.label: Sensitive Information Disclosure
security_controls.description: LLM inadvertently reveals sensitive data from training data, system prompts, or retrieved documents in its responses.
security_controls.notes: System prompt is not returned to users. RAG citations are scoped to indexed corpus. Full eval in 36-red-teaming.

security_controls.label: Account Management
security_controls.description: The organization manages information system accounts, including establishing, activating, modifying, reviewing, disabling, and removing accounts.
security_controls.notes: Entra ID group-based account management via EVA-Admins group. Admin face provides user list view (planned WI-20).

security_controls.label: Audit Events
security_controls.description: The organization determines that the information system is capable of auditing defined events.
security_controls.notes: Basic audit via Cosmos row_version + modified_by. Full append-only audit trail specced in DM-AUDIT-WI-01 (Sprint-9).

security_controls.label: Identification and Authentication (Organizational Users)
security_controls.description: The information system uniquely identifies and authenticates organizational users.
security_controls.notes: Entra ID (MSAL/OIDC) + OID-based persona resolution via eva-roles-api. All non-machine actors require valid JWT.

security_controls.label: LLM Prompt Injection via Indirect Channel
security_controls.description: Adversary embeds adversarial instructions in content processed by the LLM (e.g. retrieved documents), causing it to follow attacker instructions.
security_controls.notes: Mitigated via chunk metadata validation in the RAG pipeline. Indirect injection via case law corpus is the primary vector.

security_controls.label: Membership Inference Attack
security_controls.description: Adversary queries the model to determine whether specific data was used in training, enabling inference of sensitive information.
security_controls.notes: Membership inference eval planned in 36-red-teaming Sprint-8.

security_controls.label: AI Risk Governance Policy
security_controls.description: Policies, processes, procedures, and practices are in place to define, communicate, and enforce AI risk priorities.
security_controls.notes: AI governance gate CI workflow (eva-ai-governance-gate.yml) enforces pre-merge validation. Full policy framework pending.

security_controls.label: Protection of Information at Rest
security_controls.description: The information system protects the confidentiality and integrity of information at rest.
security_controls.notes: Azure Cosmos DB + Blob Storage use Azure-managed encryption at rest (AES-256). Private endpoints prevent public access.

risks.title: ACA image drift -- deployed image lags main branch
risks.description: ACA runs stale image if rebuild/redeploy is not triggered after schema changes. DPDCA sprint shipped L27-L30 and fp.py; ACA still on pre-DPDCA image.

risks.notes: G03/G07/G08 all FAIL due to this risk. Sprint 8 remediation in progress.

risks.title: FP classification accuracy -- EI/EO/EQ assignments unreviewed
risks.description: 76 endpoints stamped by automated script using HTTP method heuristics. Some edge cases (POST /v1/roles/check-feature as EQ) may be misclassified without expert review.
risks.mitigation: stamp-tft.ps1 classification logic documented with explicit rules. Review trigger: FP estimate UFP variance > 20% from manual count.

risks.title: Cosmos DB cost growth as model expands past 5000 objects
risks.description: Model currently at 4006 objects. Each new project layer (51 projects x 6 layers) could push to 8000+ objects. Cosmos RU consumption grows linearly.
risks.mitigation: Monitor via GET /model/agent-summary total. At 8000+ objects, consider archiving retired-project layers or tiering to cool storage.
risks.notes: Current sandbox Cosmos tier sufficient through 2026 at current growth rate.

risks.title: Model drift -- source code changes not reflected in model
risks.description: Developers may rename endpoints, add screens, or change routes without updating the model. MTI drops silently.
risks.mitigation: F37-10-004 Same-PR enforcement + F37-10-005 scheduled drift detection. Mitigated when both are shipped.
risks.notes: Currently relying on developer discipline + veritas session-start audit. Will be automated in Sprint 8.

risks.description: EVA_APIM_KEY referenced in copilot-instructions.md in plain text. If instructions file is committed, key leaks.

decisions.title: Use Cosmos DB NoSQL as the primary persistence layer
decisions.context: The data model needs a persistence layer that is 24x7 available, schemaless to accommodate evolving layers, and accessible from ACA without VNet dependencies. Options considered: PostgreSQL Flexible Server, Azure Table Storage, Cosmos DB NoSQL.
decisions.decision: Use Azure Cosmos DB NoSQL API (single container model_objects, partition key /layer) hosted on marco-sandbox-cosmos. ACA Container App reads COSMOS_URL + COSMOS_KEY from environment secrets.
decisions.consequences: All 31 layers stored in one Cosmos container. Partition key /layer enables efficient per-layer queries. RU cost scales with object count. Single-worker constraint on ACA avoids split-brain with MemoryStore.
alternatives_considered: PostgreSQL Flexible Server: rejected -- requires VNet peering or public endpoint, higher operational overhead
Azure Table Storage: rejected -- no JSON-native query, poor cross-layer graph traversal
In-memory only: rejected -- data lost on ACA restart

decisions.title: HTTP API is the sole agent interface -- no direct file reads of model/*.json
decisions.context: AI agents (Copilot, chain-of-thought loops) were reading model/*.json source files directly, resulting in 10-15 tool calls per context-gathering task. The files are implementation details of the API server and may be stale.
decisions.decision: Agents must use the HTTP API exclusively. model/*.json files are write-only artifacts produced by POST /model/admin/export. The copilot-instructions GOLDEN RULE enforces this: agents that grep model files are forbidden.
decisions.consequences: Agents need to know the ACA base URL. One HTTP call replaces 10 file reads. Model files may lag Cosmos by up to 60s (cache TTL) but are authoritative at export time.
alternatives_considered: Direct file reads: rejected -- stale, bypasses row_version, no audit trail
SDK/library: rejected -- overhead for PowerShell and CLI agents
decisions.notes: Documented in copilot-instructions.md GOLDEN RULE section and USER-GUIDE.md.

decisions.title: Use IFPUG FPA (UFP method) for story-point estimation
decisions.context: The team needed a reproducible, model-driven story-point estimation method. COSMIC, USE CASE POINTS, and IFPUG FPA were evaluated. The model already tracks containers (ILF/EIF candidates) and endpoints (EI/EO/EQ).
decisions.decision: Use IFPUG Function Point Analysis. Containers map to data function types (ILF/EIF). Endpoints map to transaction function types (EI/EO/EQ). UFP = sum of weighted components. Story points = UFP * 2.4 (COCOMO II). Effort-days = UFP * 0.5.
decisions.consequences: FP accuracy depends on DET/FTR counts per endpoint. Initial estimates use default complexity weights. As det_count/ftr_count fields are populated, accuracy improves. GET /model/fp/estimate returns live UFP.
alternatives_considered: COSMIC: rejected -- requires operational process mapping not available in current model
Story poker only: rejected -- no reproducibility, no model linkage
Use Case Points: rejected -- no use case catalog in model
decisions.notes: fp/estimate requires ACA redeploy to activate (DPDCA sprint image). TFT: EI=23 EO=25 EQ=28. DFT: ILF=12 EIF=1.

decisions.title: Add complexity_coverage as 4th MTI component (weight 0.15)
decisions.context: Three-component MTI (coverage=0.40, evidence=0.20, consistency=0.25) did not reward stories that touched complex endpoints or containers. High-complexity work was underweighted relative to trivial stories.
decisions.decision: Add complexity_coverage (weight 0.15) sourced from veritas enrich.js enrichment step. Rebalance weights: coverage=0.40, evidence=0.20, consistency=0.25, complexity=0.15. Falls back to 3-component formula when enrichment data is absent (backwards compat).
decisions.consequences: Stories lacking story_ids on endpoints will score 0 for complexity_coverage. This creates an incentive to stamp story_ids (F37-10-001). veritas audit pipeline now runs enrich() between reconcile() and computeTrust().
alternatives_considered: Keep 3-component formula: rejected -- does not differentiate complex vs trivial work
Replace consistency with complexity: rejected -- consistency catches naming drift which is valuable
decisions.notes: Activated when story_ids stamped on endpoints. Currently falls back to 3-component (MTI=86 local).
